task_completion.description: Open the Sygic GPS Navigation application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic GPS Navigation app is launched and visible on the screen (showing the map or main interface).

task_completion.description: Open the main menu or sidebar
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main navigation menu, sidebar, or dashboard of the Sygic app is expanded and visible.
The Settings menu of the Sygic app is displayed on the screen.
The 'Route computing' (or 'Route planning') option or section is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select the Economical route computing method
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Economic' or 'Economical' option is clearly selected (e.g., via a checked radio button, checkmark, or highlighted text) under the Route computing settings.

integrity.description: Agent used the graphical user interface to change the setting
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Sygic app's UI menus rather than a terminal emulator or shell executing commands.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine Sygic application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the Sygic GPS Navigation app, not a text editor modifying the com.sygic.aura_preferences.xml file directly.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply search for the task description online
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no web browsers open showing search results for 'Sygic Economical route computing'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The app package is com.sygic.aura. In the UI, the setting is typically labeled 'Route computing' and the target option is 'Economic' or 'Economical'. Internally, this corresponds to the 'PlanningSettings' key with a value of 3 in /data/data/com.sygic.aura/shared_prefs/com.sygic.aura_preferences.xml. The VLM should verify the UI state reflects the 'Economic' or 'Economical' selection.
